perturbation.original_text: 4. Contractor's Obligations 4.1 Contractor shall provide Customer with the following services: - Material planning - Material procurement - Incoming Inspection - Assembly of printed circuit boards & cables - Final assembly & integration of the Product - In Circuit test - Functional test - Packaging and delivery 4.2 Customer's production facilities Contractor will be obliged to allocate to Customer, production and storage space as well as trained production and testing personnel as an integral part of this Agreement. Contractor shall apply for and receive the ISO13488 standard for the production facility, by no later than . During the term of this Agreement, the manufacturing services provided by the Contractor hereto shall confirm in all material respects with the ISOl3488 standard.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Contractor's Obligations 4.1 Contractor shall provide Customer with the following services: - Material planning - Material procurement - Incoming Inspection - Assembly of printed circuit boards & cables - Final assembly & integration of the Product - In Circuit test - Functional test - Packaging and delivery 4.2 Customer's production facilities Contractor will be obliged to allocate to Customer, production and storage space as well as trained production and testing personnel as an integral part of this Agreement. During the term of this Agreement, the manufacturing services provided by the Contractor hereto shall confirm in all material respects with the ISOl3488 standard.
perturbation.explanation: The clause 'Contractor shall apply for and receive the ISO13488 standard for the production facility, by no later than .' has been removed. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because section 9.2 refers to 'Contractor shall maintain quality assurance standards in accordance with ISO 13488, Seller's Quality Assurance, Control and Inspection shall be in compliance with all material ISO 13488 standards during the Terms of this Agreement.' Now, without the explicit obligation in Section 4.2 to obtain the ISO13488 standard, it's unclear how Contractor can guarantee compliance as stated in Section 9.2. This creates ambiguity regarding the level of quality assurance Contractor is bound to provide.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.2 Contractor shall maintain quality assurance standards in accordance with ISO 13488, Seller's Quality Assurance, Control and Inspection shall be in compliance with all material ISO 13488 standards during the Terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 4.2 explicitly stated, 'Contractor shall apply for and receive the ISO13488 standard for the production facility, by no later than .' This obligation has been removed in the changed text. However, section 9.2 states, 'Contractor shall maintain quality assurance standards in accordance with ISO 13488...'. Without the explicit requirement to obtain the ISO13488 standard, the changed text contradicts section 9.2 because it's no longer clear how the Contractor can guarantee compliance with a standard they are not explicitly required to obtain. This creates a strong inconsistency regarding the level of quality assurance the Contractor is obligated to provide, fitting the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Material Procurement The material procurement undertaking, pursuant to this Agreement, will be carried-out by the Contractor. 6.1 Contractor is authorized to purchase materials using standard purchasing practices including, but not limited to acquisition of materials recognizing Economic Order Quantity, ABC buy policy and long lead time components management, in order to meet the requirements of Customer's orders and forecasts. 2 Source: INMODE LTD., F-1/A, 7/29/2019 6.2 Economic Order Quantity ("EOQ") for items which are un-returnable to vendor or unusable for other clients of the Contractor must be pre- approved by Customer. For such pre-approved EOQ's, Customer shall advance to the Contractor sums on account of future deliveries equal to the cost attributed to the quantity ordered exceeding the 3 months forecast. 6.3 Long Lead Items In order to manage demand fluctuations Contractor shall suggest from time to time a list of LLI's to be approved by the Customer. Contractor shall maintain in inventory certain quantities of LLI. "LLI" shall mean Long Lead Item materials required in order to complete manufacture and supply of Products. For the avoidance of doubt Customer shall have no additional liability with respect to the holding of LLI other than the liability provided herein below. The usage of LLI by Contractor for the manufacture of Items shall be by a written order by Customer in accordance with this Agreement, stipulating the quantity Customer wishes Contractor to use. If Contractor holds LLIs based on any written requirement for more than three (3) months, Customer shall be required to purchase such LLIs at their direct costs plus a handling fee of 2% of Product price. The purchase terms of such LLI's, set forth in Section 8.2 hereof. 6.4 Contractor is responsible for monitoring supplier's quality, according to the Specifications provided by Customer for all purchased materials.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Material Procurement The material procurement undertaking, pursuant to this Agreement, will be carried-out by the Contractor. 6.1 Contractor is authorized to purchase materials using standard purchasing practices including, but not limited to acquisition of materials recognizing Economic Order Quantity, ABC buy policy and long lead time components management, in order to meet the requirements of Customer's orders and forecasts. 2 Source: INMODE LTD., F-1/A, 7/29/2019 6.2 Economic Order Quantity ("EOQ") for items which are un-returnable to vendor or unusable for other clients of the Contractor must be pre- approved by Customer. For such pre-approved EOQ's, Customer shall advance to the Contractor sums on account of future deliveries equal to the cost attributed to the quantity ordered exceeding the 3 months forecast. 6.3 Long Lead Items In order to manage demand fluctuations Contractor shall suggest from time to time a list of LLI's to be approved by the Customer. Contractor shall maintain in inventory certain quantities of LLI. "LLI" shall mean Long Lead Item materials required in order to complete manufacture and supply of Products. For the avoidance of doubt Customer shall have no additional liability with respect to the holding of LLI other than the liability provided herein below. The usage of LLI by Contractor for the manufacture of Items shall be by a written order by Customer in accordance with this Agreement, stipulating the quantity Customer wishes Contractor to use. If Contractor holds LLIs based on any written requirement for more than three (3) months, Customer shall be required to purchase such LLIs at their direct costs plus a handling fee of 2% of Product price.
perturbation.explanation: The sentence 'The purchase terms of such LLI's, set forth in Section 8.2 hereof.' has been omitted. This creates a contradiction because Section 6.3 now refers to purchase terms that are supposedly in Section 8.2. However, Section 8.2 actually discusses Customer's liability for cancelled or delayed Purchase Orders, and it does not contain any terms related to the purchase of LLI. This creates an in-text contradiction because now section 6.3 makes reference to something, that doesn't exist in section 8.2, creating uncertainty about the purchasing process and which section to refer to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.3 Long Lead Items In order to manage demand fluctuations Contractor shall suggest from time to time a list of LLI's to be approved by the Customer. Contractor shall maintain in inventory certain quantities of LLI. "LLI" shall mean Long Lead Item materials required in order to complete manufacture and supply of Products. For the avoidance of doubt Customer shall have no additional liability with respect to the holding of LLI other than the liability provided herein below. The usage of LLI by Contractor for the manufacture of Items shall be by a written order by Customer in accordance with this Agreement, stipulating the quantity Customer wishes Contractor to use. If Contractor holds LLIs based on any written requirement for more than three (3) months, Customer shall be required to purchase such LLIs at their direct costs plus a handling fee of 2% of Product price.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the sentence 'The purchase terms of such LLI's, set forth in Section 8.2 hereof.' in Section 6.3 creates an in-text contradiction. The remaining text in Section 6.3 refers to purchase terms that are now supposed to be found in Section 8.2. However, Section 8.2 does not contain any terms related to the purchase of LLI, leading to uncertainty and a direct conflict as to where those terms can be found. This is a strong contradiction because Section 6.3 explicitly directs the reader to Section 8.2 for information that is not there, rendering the section unclear and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Delivery and Inspection, Title and shipping 12.1 Contractor undertakes to report to Customer once (1) a week, or per Customer request, the quantity of Products ready for delivery 12.2 Customer will notify Contractor, from time to time, quantities of Products and destinations to which to ship the Products. 12.3 If the delivery destination is within Israel, excluding port/airport ("Limited Delivery Territory") than the delivery shall be made by Contractor at no additional cost and to such destination of delivery Contractor shall incur insurance transport costs. Upon delivery or the placement of an invoice by Contractor, whichever is earlier, Risk of loss and title will pass to Customer. 12.4 The price for Deliveries to other destinations outside the Limited Delivery Territory, including for export will be agreed by the parties. All risk of loss, responsibility and cost shall be borne by the Customer Ex-Factory. 12.5 To each delivery, Contractor shall include all required documentation (e.g. bill of lading, QA/QC certificate). Upon delivery to Customer, Customer will sign the bill of lading. Such signature shall only be deemed as acknowledgement of receipt of the delivery and not confirmation as to the delivered Products' condition and quality. 7 Source: INMODE LTD., F-1/A, 7/29/2019 12.6 Subject to the above limitations, the Contractor will ship and deliver the Products according to Customer's instructions in the best and safest means of transportation, to the extent commercially reasonable.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Delivery and Inspection, Title and shipping 12.1 Contractor undertakes to report to Customer once (1) a week, or per Customer request, the quantity of Products ready for delivery 12.2 Customer will notify Contractor, from time to time, quantities of Products and destinations to which to ship the Products. 12.3 If the delivery destination is within Israel, excluding port/airport ("Limited Delivery Territory") than the delivery shall be made by Contractor at no additional cost and to such destination of delivery Contractor shall incur insurance transport costs. Upon delivery or the placement of an invoice by Contractor, whichever is earlier, Risk of loss and title will pass to Customer. 12.4 The price for Deliveries to other destinations outside the Limited Delivery Territory, including for export will be agreed by the parties. All risk of loss, responsibility and cost shall be borne by the Customer Ex-Factory. 12.5 To each delivery, Contractor shall include all required documentation (e.g. bill of lading, QA/QC certificate). Upon delivery to Customer, Customer will sign the bill of lading. Such signature shall only be deemed as acknowledgement of receipt of the delivery and not confirmation as to the delivered Products' condition and quality. 12.6 Subject to the above limitations, the Contractor will ship and deliver the Products according to Customer's instructions.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'in the best and safest means of transportation, to the extent commercially reasonable' has been omitted. This removal creates an in-text contradiction with Section 12.3. Section 12.3 specifies that within the 'Limited Delivery Territory,' the Contractor incurs insurance transport costs, implying a responsibility for safe delivery. By removing the obligation to use the 'best and safest means of transportation' in Section 12.6, it becomes unclear what standard of care the Contractor must exercise, potentially conflicting with the implicit responsibility suggested in Section 12.3 regarding insurance and transport costs within the Limited Delivery Territory. The contractor will still have to deliver the products, but not in the safest way, possibly incurring in extra costs due to damaged products.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.6 Subject to the above limitations, the Contractor will ship and deliver the Products according to Customer's instructions.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the Contractor would deliver products 'in the best and safest means of transportation, to the extent commercially reasonable'. The changed text omits this phrase, now stating simply that 'the Contractor will ship and deliver the Products according to Customer's instructions.' This omission contradicts the implicit responsibility for safe delivery suggested in Section 12.3 regarding insurance and transport costs within the Limited Delivery Territory. The contradicted text, which is the same section 12.6 after the change, now requires the contractor to deliver the product without specifying it needs to be done so safely, which poses a contradiction to section 12.3, thus leading to a contradiction.
